decisions.summary: Beads created for PAN-956: workspace-ez7o (cleanup guard), workspace-gl3h (stable path), workspace-04p9 (re-render), workspace-kj8w (doctor check)

Implemented workspace-ez7o: Added getContainersReferencingWorkspacePath() helper, modified stopWorkspaceDocker to handle orphaned containers via project-name down or raw docker stop/rm, guarded removeWorktree and removeWorkspace and deep-wipe endpoint from deleting workspaces with active container references.

hazards.summary: Docker containers may hold references to deleted compose paths, breaking compose operations
hazards.mitigation: Add guards before deleting .devcontainer/ and implement doctor check for auto-recovery

resumePoint.description: Claim and implement bead workspace-gl3h: Ensure rendered compose path is stable and protected during cleanup. Need to ensure .devcontainer/ path is stable and documented, and verify cleanup guards are comprehensive.

beadsMapping.workspace-ez7o: Add explicit cleanup guard for rendered compose files
beadsMapping.workspace-gl3h: Ensure rendered compose path is stable and protected during cleanup
beadsMapping.workspace-04p9: Ensure container recreation re-renders compose files
beadsMapping.workspace-kj8w: Add pan doctor check for compose label/path mismatch

sessionHistory.note: Initialized PAN-956 workspace. Issue is about Docker container labels referencing missing rendered compose paths. Created 4 beads and preparing to implement workspace-ez7o first.

Completed workspace-ez7o: Added getContainersReferencingWorkspacePath helper, modified stopWorkspaceDocker to handle orphaned containers, guarded removeWorktree/removeWorkspace/deep-wipe from deleting workspaces with active container references. All tests pass.
